content.intro: A Section 8 notice is used when seeking possession of a property due to a breach of the tenancy agreement, most commonly rent arrears. PropertyGoose helps you generate and track these notices.

steps.title: Open the tenancy
steps.content: Navigate to Tenancies and click on the tenancy requiring the notice.

steps.title: Click 'Section 8 Notice'
steps.content: In the tenancy detail view, click the 'Section 8 Notice' button from the actions menu.

steps.title: Select grounds
steps.content: Choose the applicable grounds for possession. Ground 8 (mandatory) and Grounds 10/11 (discretionary) are common for rent arrears.

steps.title: Enter arrears details
steps.content: Input the current arrears amount and any relevant dates.

steps.title: Generate and serve
steps.content: Click 'Generate Notice' to create the document. You must then serve it to the tenant according to legal requirements.

sections.title: Legal Advice Recommended
sections.content: Section 8 notices have strict legal requirements. We strongly recommend seeking legal advice before serving a notice to ensure it's valid and enforceable.

sections.title: Notice Periods
sections.content: Different grounds require different notice periods. Ground 8 (serious arrears) requires 2 weeks notice. Grounds 10 and 11 require 2 months notice. PropertyGoose calculates the correct dates for you.

sections.title: Tracking Notices
sections.content: All notices are stored in the tenancy's document history. You can track when notices were generated, served, and when the notice period expires.
